response:
## Confirmation of duplication

`crimes scan` surfaces two consolidation targets in this fixture (plus a related role-check and clock-coupling problem):

| Finding | Type | Bodies | Files |
|---|---|---|---|
| `crime_00002` | near-duplicate block (3×, conf 0.85) | `sanitizeUserInput` / `sanitizeText` / `cleanMessage` — identical logic, only the local variable name (`working` / `cleaned` / `normalised`) and the param name differ | `src/sanitize-a.ts:10-36`, `src/sanitize-b.ts:9-35`, `src/sanitize-c.ts:9-35` |
| `crime_00001` | exact duplicate block (2×, conf 0.95) | `recordSanitiseEvent` — byte-identical | `src/telemetry-a.ts:6-19`, `src/telemetry-b.ts:6-19` |
| `crime_00003` | duplicated `"admin"` check across 4 files, 2 expression shapes (`role === "admin"` vs `role !== "admin"` vs `role === "admin" \|\| role === "owner"`) | — | `src/policy.ts`, `src/sanitize-{a,b,c}.ts` |
| `crime_00004/5` | `Date.now()` called inline inside the duplicated telemetry function | — | `src/telemetry-{a,b}.ts:16` |

The three sanitize bodies are character-for-character identical once you alpha-rename the local. They apply the same pipeline: null-guard → coerce → trim → strip control chars → collapse whitespace → 1000-char cap → reject leading `<`/`>` → reject `\x` escapes → reject `!?` runs (>5) → lowercase if >90% uppercase and >30 chars.

## Proposed refactor

Collapse into a single helper module, route every call site through it, and fix the two adjacent crimes (admin policy + clock) while you're in there — they're the reason this duplication is dangerous, not just ugly.

**New files**
- `src/sanitize.ts` — the one true sanitizer + the role policy.
- `src/telemetry.ts` — the single `recordSanitiseEvent`, with an injectable clock.

**Files that change**
- **Delete** `src/sanitize-a.ts`, `src/sanitize-b.ts`, `src/sanitize-c.ts`, `src/telemetry-a.ts`, `src/telemetry-b.ts`.
- **Update** `src/policy.ts` and every importer of `sanitizeUserInput` / `sanitizeText` / `cleanMessage` / `assertAdminA|B|C` / `recordSanitiseEvent` to import from the new modules. (`crimes context src/sanitize-a.ts` will list these — worth running before the rename so nothing is missed.)
- **Keep** `src/policy.ts` only if it re-exports from `sanitize.ts`; otherwise fold it in.

## Public API of the consolidated functions

```ts
// src/sanitize.ts

/**
 * Sanitize user-supplied text for safe downstream use.
 * Returns "" for null/undefined/over-budget/structurally rejected input.
 * Pure: no I/O, no clock, no globals.
 */
export function sanitizeText(input: string | null | undefined): string;

/** Tunables that today are hard-coded in the duplicated bodies. */
export interface SanitizeOptions {
  maxLength?: number;          // default 1000
  maxPunctuationRuns?: number; // default 5  (runs of /[!?]{3,}/)
  lowercaseIfShoutyOver?: number; // default 30 chars
  shoutyUpperRatio?: number;   // default 0.9
}
export function sanitizeText(input: string | null | undefined, opts?: SanitizeOptions): string;

// src/policy.ts (or merged into sanitize.ts)

/** Single source of truth for the "admin" check. */
export type Role = "admin" | "owner" | "member" | string;
export function isAdmin(role: Role): boolean;       // replaces assertAdminA, the === "admin" sites in policy.ts
export function isAdminOrOwner(role: Role): boolean; // replaces assertAdminB
// assertAdminC's `role !== "admin"` becomes `!isAdmin(role)` at call sites — don't ship a negated variant.
```

```ts
// src/telemetry.ts

export interface Clock { now(): number; }
export const systemClock: Clock = { now: () => Date.now() };

/**
 * Emits a "sanitise.complete" event. Clock is injectable so tests can pin time
 * without monkey-patching Date.now (closes crime_00004 / crime_00005).
 */
export function recordSanitiseEvent(
  originalLength: number,
  sanitisedLength: number,
  clock?: Clock, // defaults to systemClock
): void;
```

## Why this shape

- **One `sanitizeText`, not three.** The three names (`sanitizeUserInput` / `sanitizeText` / `cleanMessage`) are the smell — pick the most neutral and delete the others. `crimes` flagged this as `agent_risk = 0.62` precisely because an agent fixing a bug in one copy will miss the other two.
- **Options object instead of magic numbers.** The duplicated bodies hard-code `1000`, `5`, `30`, `0.9`. Defaulted options preserve current behaviour at every call site while making the knobs visible.
- **`isAdmin` / `isAdminOrOwner`, no `assertAdminC`.** Two policies, not three. Negation is the caller's job (`!isAdmin(role)`) — shipping `notAdmin` is how `crime_00003`'s "2 distinct expression shapes" happens again in six months.
- **Clock injection on the telemetry function.** Cheaper to fix now (one file) than after the duplicate is reintroduced. Defaulted to `systemClock` so existing call sites don't change.

## Suggested verification

After the refactor, re-run `crimes scan --format json` on the fixture — `crime_00001`, `crime_00002`, `crime_00003`, `crime_00004`, `crime_00005` should all drop out, taking `summary.total` from 5 to 0.
